sections.paragraph:
-

(1) to twist, to twine like a rope (kindred to the roots שׁוּר, טוּר, סוּר, זוּר, דּוּר; all of which have the idea of turning, twisting, going in a circle, variously inflected). Hence שֹׁר and שֹׁרֶר the navel (pr. the umbilical cord; Nabelftrang); שְׁרִירִים nerves, sinews; שֵׁרָה, שַׁרְשְׁרָה, שַׁרְשָׁה a chain (as if a rope made of metal). Hence-

(2) to be firm, hard (Syr. Pael, to make firm, stable); especially in a bad sense; whence שְׁרִירוּת obstinacy (of heart).

(3) to press together; hence to oppress, to treat as an enemy, i.q. צָרַר No. 4. Part. שֹׁרֵר an adversary, an enemy, Psa 27:11 54:7 56:3 59:11 .

Derivatives, see Kal No. 1, 2.
